title: SentinelMesh AI cybersecurity SaaS product video: brand-graded demo cut with 30s promo + 15s teaser cutdowns and on-brand callout / end-card graphic plates
one_line_ask: Turn SentinelMesh's raw screen recordings, product b-roll, logo, and brand color reference into a polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity SaaS demo cut plus a 30s promo cutdown and 15s teaser (with a vertical social reframe), and build on-brand lower-third / call-out / graded end-card graphic plates and a vector logo as the editable-asset set for the Premiere handoff.

issues: Photograph of a sticker on paper, not a direct digital asset (acceptable as a source input per brief)
Slight tilt and off-white background (allowed per brief)
Some minor dust/marks visible on the sticker (acceptable for source input)
Sticker gloss/shine present (not an issue for source input)
Slight perspective tilt on the notebook surface (within acceptable <=5deg threshold for source asset)
Transparent sticker carrier film shows minor reflective sheen at top-right corner (acceptable for source input)

Logo mark uses a gradient-like teal-to-dark-navy transition in the shield icon rather than strict flat single teal — minor deviation from pure flat single-color spec, but acceptable as starting asset for designer to flatten
Background is off-white textured cardboard surface rather than pure transparent background, but this is the physical mockup presentation layer, not the logo file itself

issues: Slight over-polished rendering quality edges toward high-end CGI composite rather than pure photographic capture, though remains within acceptable photoreal threshold
Monitor text/UI elements are too small and low-resolution to confirm fictional vs real branding
Background window display (top-right) shows data tables that could be mistaken for real financial/security software UI — unverifiable at this resolution
Coffee cup has no visible branding (acceptable)
Desk lamp arm slightly clips monitor bezel on left edge — minor compositional tightness
